assertion_results.text: The conversation key derivation uses ECDH (secp256k1 scalar multiplication) to compute a shared point, using the raw unhashed x-coordinate as IKM

assertion_results.text: Per-message keys are derived using HKDF-expand with the conversation_key as PRK, nonce as info, and output length 76 bytes, split into chacha_key(32), chacha_nonce(12), hmac_key(32)
assertion_results.evidence: No matches for "length 76 bytes, split into chacha_key(32), chacha_nonce(12), hmac_key(32)" found across 1 output file(s)

assertion_results.text: The nonce is 32 bytes generated from a CSPRNG (crypto.getRandomValues or equivalent)

assertion_results.text: The padding implementation uses a power-of-2 based algorithm with minimum padded size of 32 bytes and plaintext length encoded as 2-byte big-endian prefix
assertion_results.evidence:

  nip44.ts:77: // Step 3: Power-of-2 Padding

assertion_results.text: The MAC is computed as HMAC-SHA256 with the nonce concatenated before the ciphertext as AAD
